title: Merge Intervals
description:
Given a list of intervals `intervals`, where each interval is `[start, end]`, merge every pair of intervals that overlap (touching endpoints count as overlapping, e.g. `[1,4]` and `[4,5]` merge into `[1,5]`). Return the merged intervals sorted by start.

**Example**

```
intervals = [[1,3],[2,6],[8,10],[15,18]]  ->  [[1,6],[8,10],[15,18]]
```
hints: Sort intervals by start first — once sorted, only ever-adjacent intervals in the list can possibly overlap.

Walk the sorted list keeping a 'current merged interval'; if the next interval's start is <= the current merged end, extend the end (max of the two ends); otherwise close out the current interval and start a new one.

solutions.name: Sort then linear merge
solutions.explanation: Sort intervals by start so any overlap must occur between neighbors in the sorted order. Keep a running merged interval; for each subsequent interval, if its start is at or before the running interval's end, they overlap so extend the end to the max of the two; otherwise the running interval is finished, so save it and start a new running interval.
solutions.complexity: Time O(n log n), Space O(n)
